demographics.living_situation: at home; was alone when the symptoms started

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was brushing my teeth and saw these white webs all over the inside of my cheeks. I googled it, and I'm having a panic attack because it looks exactly like mouth cancer.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I found these white webs in my mouth about two hours ago and I'm terrified it's cancer — and now my throat feels like it's closing up.

symptom_narrative.onset: noticed the white webs about two hours ago while brushing my teeth; the tight-throat feeling and racing heart started right after I saw them and started reading online
symptom_narrative.character: painless white lacy webs or streaks inside both cheeks, plus a panicky tight feeling in my throat and a pounding, racing heart
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the mouth thing doesn't hurt at all, but I feel like I'm panicking — my heart is pounding and my throat feels tight
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: inside both cheeks; the tight feeling is in my throat
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since I noticed it, and it gets worse every time I look in the mirror or read about it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing yet — I keep checking my mouth with a flashlight
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: looking at it and searching my symptoms online makes the panic worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: racing heart, breathing fast, and a feeling like my throat is closing — but I can still swallow and talk normally
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've never noticed these white lines before, but I do tend to get very anxious and have had health scares like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't stop checking my mouth and I'm too anxious to focus on anything else

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: I worry a lot about my health and have had panicky health scares before
past_medical_history.how_managed: no formal treatment that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: was alone at home when this started and had been searching my symptoms on the internet

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants definitive certainty that this is not cancer and that the throat sensation is not dangerous

communication_style.typical_message_style: rapid, frightened messages, repeatedly seeking reassurance and describing checking the mouth again

objective_data_offered.value: white lacy web-like streaks inside both cheeks that I can see in the mirror; they don't hurt
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own description in the mirror; not a clinician oral examination

objective_data_offered.value: white lacy streaks visible on the inner cheeks when I hold the phone camera and a light up to my open mouth
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: phone-camera view of the mouth in poor light; cannot replace an in-person oral examination

objective_data_offered.value: I rubbed the white lines with my finger and my tongue and they don't wipe or scrape off
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-performed, not a clinician-performed scrape test

objective_data_offered.value: my smartwatch says my heart rate is about 110
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single wearable reading taken while acutely distressed

objective_data_offered.value: I'm breathing fast and my heart is pounding
